title: Two sum
prompt:
Given a list of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return the **indices** of the two numbers that add up to `target`, as a list sorted ascending.

Exactly one solution exists; you may not use the same element twice.

**Example**: `two_sum([2, 7, 11, 15], 9)` → `[0, 1]`

Aim for **O(n)** — an interviewer will push back on the nested-loop version.
hint_notes: Hash map of value->index, one pass. Tag if O(n^2): missed-hashmap-tradeoff.
